title: YC handoff demo needs CLI-first recording proof
summary: For the YC/team memory demo, a browser rendered product explainer is not enough when the ask is to record Codex and Claude CLI usage. Keep a CLI first artifact that shows terminal prompts, Codex output, Kage recall/code
body:
For the YC/team-memory demo, a browser-rendered product explainer is not enough when the ask is to record Codex and Claude CLI usage. Keep a CLI-first artifact that shows terminal prompts, Codex output, Kage recall/code graph facts, Claude output, and metrics from the real transcripts. Native macOS screen capture is blocked in this environment, so the practical fallback is a terminal-only video rendered from PTY/script logs, not a Chrome/product-page wrapper.
Evidence: screencapture still image failed with 'could not create image from display'. Existing real proof logs are /tmp/kage-codex-recall-proof.log and /tmp/kage-claude-recall-proof.log. Added scripts/render-cli-recording.mjs and rendered docs/assets/kage-cli-recording.mp4; ffprobe verified 1600x900, 25.2s, 252 frames.
Verified by: screencapture failure, script proof logs, ffprobe
